title: puccinellia langeana
intro: Puccinellia langeana, also known as Lange's alkali grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and acting as a diuretic. Puccinellia langeana can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
